121673.explanation: We support good participation and residents must be able to talk and participate in decision-making. However, a referendum is often a yes/no question and thus black and white. Especially in a small municipality, we must organize participation well and truly listen to residents.

121674.explanation: The municipality should not shift responsibilities, but sometimes management can lead to more togetherness and social cohesion. In that case, the municipality should be supportive and able to transfer management of, for example, a piece of greenery to residents.

121677.explanation: We believe the municipality should support residents who want to start sustainable initiatives. The Ouder-Amstel Energy cooperative is a good example.

121678.explanation: There is limited space in our municipality and we see no place for a distribution center. Fortunately, this is not an issue.

121680.explanation: This depends on the situation and, in our opinion, does not apply in our municipality.

121682.explanation: In Ouder-Amstel, we have many places where residents can burn wood without nuisance. In the village centers, we should discourage wood burning through information, especially at times (e.g., during fog) when it causes significant nuisance.

121684.explanation: National policy allows associations to set off fireworks. All enforcement resources are needed for this. We cannot use these for a fireworks show. Moreover, it costs a lot of money.

121685.explanation: Currently, bulky waste can be collected upon request. It is also possible to bring bulky waste to the recycling center. This system works well.

121688.explanation: In specific cases, businesses must be allowed to be converted into homes.

121689.explanation: Sports, in our view, is a basic facility that should be accessible to everyone. It also increases happiness and fitness, which leads to lower costs in other areas.

121691.explanation: We meet the legal obligation to ensure good housing for schools as a municipality. We know funds are needed for the renovation/new construction of Amstelschool and 't Kofschip.

121692.explanation: Arts and cultural education, such as drawing, music, dance, and theater lessons, should be accessible to everyone, including people with low incomes. However, subsidies are not necessary for those who can afford music lessons themselves.

121696.explanation: Visible and approachable enforcement officers increase the feeling of safety in neighborhoods. They can respond quickly to nuisance, work preventively, and maintain contact with residents. Together, we ensure a safe and pleasant living environment in Ouder-Amstel.

121697.explanation: Camera surveillance can help in exceptional situations, but is not a solution by itself. We focus mainly on prevention, good lighting, and visible enforcement officers. If camera surveillance is really needed at a specific location, we are open to tailor-made solutions.

121698.explanation: Everyone has the right to safety. Victims of domestic violence must have quick and low-threshold access to protection and help in their own region. A shelter can save lives and offers peace and perspective. This is how Ouder-Amstel takes responsibility for safety.

121701.explanation: Permanent residence in holiday homes increases inequality. It leads to uncertain housing forms with less protection and often high costs. Everyone has the right to a full, affordable home, not an emergency solution without security.

121702.explanation: Building outside village boundaries damages the green landscape and increases pressure on nature and recreation. We choose to build within existing cores where facilities are already present. This protects Ouder-Amstel's open and valuable countryside.

121705.explanation: More and more people struggle to make ends meet. We remain critical of income limits. Although we primarily want to reduce bureaucracy-making it easier and more humane to ask for help and reach everyone-raising income limits in certain areas is not taboo.

121707.explanation: Ouder-Amstel already scores above the national average for charging points. Therefore, additional designation is not automatically necessary. If shortages appear in specific places, we will gladly work on this in a targeted way.

121709.explanation: Good care is care that suits you. People must feel seen and respected, especially in vulnerable situations. Freedom of choice for a caregiver that aligns with one's faith, culture, or beliefs contributes to trust, quality, and dignity in care.

121711.explanation: Because the WOZ value is high in our municipality, the property tax (ozb) is also high. However, the ozb is in line with neighboring municipalities. We continue to critically monitor costs for residents but plan to let the ozb rise along with inflation.

121712.explanation: The costs of collecting dog tax are high compared to the revenue. Furthermore, we believe this tax is outdated.

121715.explanation: We believe those with the greatest housing problems should get priority. Refugees certainly deserve priority, but this also applies to people with other problems.

121719.explanation: We think it is important that every child receives swimming lessons but do not find it necessary that this happens during school hours. So municipal funds should go to swimming lessons (only for children from families who cannot afford them), but not to school swimming.

121722.explanation: There is no place for high-rise buildings within existing residential cores, but there is outside them. Current residents will be informed timely and fairly about the future skyline. Perhaps by repositioning buildings or planting greenery and trees the view can be improved.

121723.explanation: Rules for cohabitation and hospitability rentals will be relaxed. Renting part of your home via Airbnb remains possible under existing regulations, but permanently renting homes as bed & breakfasts will be subject to stricter rules.

121725.explanation: The municipality has almost no paid parking but operates a permit system and blue zones. This will remain for now. Paid parking will be introduced in new residential areas. Residents must park on their own property or in garages.

121728.explanation: We support the principle that waste separation should pay off. Cards (diftar) are one way to achieve this. We want to reduce nuisance by providing more containers with an opening flap and automatic recharging of the waste card (or pay afterwards). We will address nuisance (e.g., in the Zonnehof neighborhood).

121729.explanation: We invest more in cycling so residents leave their cars behind more often. Building better bike paths and tunnels is part of this. A good connection between De Nieuwe Kern and Duivendrecht is certainly needed, but it does not have to be a tunnel.

121673.explanation: We believe that a referendum should only be held in exceptional cases. For other important matters, a well-organized participation process should be sufficient to hear the opinions of residents.

121674.explanation: We do not think it is wise to delegate the management of, for example, a sports hall or park to residents. Who would then make the policies and how consistently would they be implemented? Also, selecting which resident is allowed to manage it seems like a very difficult task for the municipality.

121677.explanation: Initiatives related to sustainability should be approached positively by the municipality. Careful consideration must be given to how much financial support is requested and what the return on jointly generated sustainable energy will be.

121678.explanation: When applying for the establishment of new distribution centers, a thorough assessment must be made of their usefulness and necessity. The municipality should not hinder economic developments.

121680.explanation: When employers in agriculture and horticulture attract seasonal workers, it seems logical to us that they also provide housing. This does not necessarily have to be on their own company grounds. If there are reasons forcing the employment of seasonal workers, then the municipality can facilitate this.

121682.explanation: Considering sustainability, which we as a municipality find very important, we think it is wise to prohibit the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves. However, we must consider how to enforce this and whether there is capacity to do so.

121684.explanation: Our municipality has three centers, so a fireworks show should be organized in each center. You cannot expect residents to travel to another center around midnight. Also consider the risk of accidents due to slippery conditions and alcohol use.

121685.explanation: This is something our faction has wanted for years. It significantly reduces litter around garbage containers. Moreover, it also benefits residents who are unable to drive to a recycling center or don’t know anyone who can do it for them.

121688.explanation: This does not seem a wise decision to us. After all, the character is agricultural and should not change into a residential area. The municipality must apply a strict selection for applications and carefully examine the justification for an additional home.

121689.explanation: Residents from this target group should be informed by the municipality about the youth sports and culture fund. The municipality can also assist with the application if needed. Good communication about this on the website and in the weekly newspaper is very important.

121691.explanation: No money should be spent on other schools. It is important that two schools in Ouderkerk, after years of waiting, receive new construction or renovation. All schools are under school boards who must take their responsibility.

121692.explanation: Good policy must be made for this because one music lesson is more expensive than another. We believe everyone should receive the same subsidy percentage. Music lessons are important for the development of primary school children and the municipality should encourage this.

121696.explanation: The capacity of enforcement officers we currently have is far too low. As a result, many issues cannot be addressed, which is not good. Seeing enforcement officers also increases the feeling of safety. Intervention in incidents or nuisance is then possible.

121697.explanation: More camera surveillance in our municipality would be nice, but is often difficult due to privacy concerns. There are places in our municipality where cameras could have a preventive effect. However, we consider it a requirement that only the police may view the footage.

121698.explanation: Victims of domestic violence must of course be able to be accommodated in a shelter. This possibility already exists at a regional level. If the municipality wants to initiate this itself, it must be able to map how many victims there are within the municipality. Vacancy is pointless.

121701.explanation: This does not seem like a sensible plan to us. Many holiday homes in our municipality are occupied by residents from other places. Facilities are also often closed during certain periods of the year, making residence impossible.

121702.explanation: Good discussions must take place in regional consultations about developments outside existing village boundaries. The municipality must also be well informed about these developments so that objections can be raised on time if necessary.

121705.explanation: If we raise the income limit to qualify for poverty assistance, more residents will get into trouble. We currently maintain a realistic income threshold so that we can provide the help our residents need.

121707.explanation: The municipality must first determine if there is a need for this and in which locations. Then it can consider whether there are possibilities for electric car charging points in those locations. A survey or participation process can help with this.

121709.explanation: A caregiver who matches one’s religion, culture, or beliefs is very important. Of course, it is also important that this caregiver is available when needed.

121711.explanation: The municipality currently applies a well-considered property tax and can adjust it through indexing. It should not be the case that property tax is used to cover possible budget shortfalls.

121712.explanation: This is something we have wanted for years because dog owners see nothing in return - no off-leash areas, poop bags, or other facilities. It cannot be that this tax goes to the general reserves and is used for other purposes.

121715.explanation: Refugees with a residence permit should be treated the same as our own residents. So they should build up housing points and receive housing allocations based on that. Currently, our own residents sometimes wait fifteen years before their turn comes.

121719.explanation: Many schools lack the staff capacity to organize this. Also, parents often already arrange swimming lessons for their children themselves. If the municipality allocates money for this, it would miss the point.

121722.explanation: No more high residential towers should be built in Ouder-Amstel. We must ensure the village character is preserved. Only if building a high residential tower is necessary to reduce noise from the motorway or metro should it be critically considered.

121723.explanation: It is often difficult to find a new home after a divorce. It is then nice to have the option to rent a room temporarily. Since the financial situation often does not allow finding a house, a ban on renting out individual rooms would be disadvantageous.

121725.explanation: If we make parking fees cheaper, it will lead to parkers from surrounding areas. This will cause nuisance for our municipality and could harm local businesses.

121728.explanation: This seems like a good proposal because since the introduction of the waste card, litter has drastically increased and residents more often complain about pests. These situations cause great annoyance among residents. This is because the municipality does not know how to handle the problem.

121729.explanation: Constructing a new bike tunnel will cost the municipality a lot of money and there are other access routes available. The risk exists that the bike tunnel will not be used optimally.

121673.explanation: Council members should listen carefully to residents and good participation is needed for major decisions. Referenda are then unnecessary and also cost a lot of money that would be better spent on municipal investments.

121674.explanation: The municipality should encourage volunteer work. Management and maintenance of public spaces or the municipal sports hall must be suitable for this. That is not always the case.

121677.explanation: No municipal subsidy is needed for this. Technologies pay for themselves. Where that is not the case, national or provincial subsidies are often already available.

121678.explanation: Distribution centers can actually help reduce truck traffic in villages by using small vehicles for the last mile.

121680.explanation: Housing must be well arranged. The farmyards in our municipality are located in polders with an open character that do not always lend themselves to additional housing.

121682.explanation: The VVD is against nannying on this. However, attention must be paid to proper use of fireplaces so that they do not cause nuisance.

121684.explanation: An alternative must be organized now that there is a fireworks ban. A joint event can contribute to community spirit and prevent unrest.

121685.explanation: That degrades the street scene. Municipal yards should facilitate residents by having extended opening hours so they can dispose of bulky waste there.

121688.explanation: Farmers are subject to the same rules as everyone else regarding housing construction. If it fits within the environmental plan, it is allowed.

121689.explanation: There are arrangements available for people who cannot afford sports clubs. The VVD continues those arrangements.

121691.explanation: The VVD invests heavily in school buildings. However, not all school buildings need it.

121692.explanation: Full subsidies are not the responsibility of the municipality. However, the municipality must facilitate that music lessons can be given at low costs.

121696.explanation: Certainly. Also in the evenings and on weekends. Enforcement officers are far too rarely visible in our municipality.

121697.explanation: It is important to be able to pay extra attention to safety at key points with camera surveillance.

121698.explanation: If there is a need for it, that space must certainly be available. The safety of residents is paramount.

121701.explanation: Holiday homes are meant for recreation.

121702.explanation: It depends on where the boundaries are drawn. There are still places where housing construction is perfectly possible so that specific homes for young people and elderly from our municipality can be built. However, this certainly cannot be everywhere.

121705.explanation: The municipality has already raised the limit. As far as the VVD is concerned, we ensure that everyone living in poverty can make optimal use of the available opportunities. Further expansion is not necessary for that purpose.

121707.explanation: That should be based on need and is already done that way.

121711.explanation: This has been considerably increased in recent years due to rising house prices. For more and more people, these charges are becoming too much. The municipal finances are healthy, so further increases are not necessary.

121712.explanation: The dog tax must be used for off-leash areas and enforcement to ensure that nuisance caused by dogs (dog poop and running loose outside permitted areas) is prevented.

121715.explanation: There are many residents who have also been waiting for years.

121719.explanation: Only for people who cannot afford it.

121722.explanation: Flats will be built in the coming years in De Nieuwe Kern and Amstel Business Park. There is little or no space for this in the existing village centers.

121723.explanation: That varies depending on the situation.

121725.explanation: This is a strange statement. Paid parking is not everywhere, and as far as we are concerned, it should not be introduced. The municipality has good experiences with blue zones, and we can apply them more often if there are problems.

121728.explanation: Abolishing the waste pass would increase waste processing costs because waste would be less well sorted and commercial waste would be dumped more often. Abolition would thus cause costs to rise and consequently increase waste charges. The VVD wants to keep local taxes low.

121729.explanation: Good cycling connections are urgently needed.

121673.explanation: Residents must be able to participate in major decisions. That strengthens trust.

121674.explanation: Citizen initiatives deserve space and support, provided they are well organized.

121677.explanation: Local initiatives deserve support, for example for solar panels on roofs.

121678.explanation: They cause extra traffic and little local added value.

121680.explanation: Employers have their own responsibility in this.

121682.explanation: Health comes first; restrictions on nuisance are logical.

121684.explanation: Safer and less nuisance for people and animals.

121685.explanation: Part of a fair waste policy.

121688.explanation: Only with clear added value and landscape integration.

121689.explanation: Sport promotes health and social connection.

121697.explanation: Targeted deployment where needed, no mass control.

121701.explanation: If it can help with the housing shortage, we must allow it.

121702.explanation: New construction is needed due to the housing shortage, but no 70-meter high-rise towers.

121705.explanation: Prevents people from falling through the cracks.

121707.explanation: Because there must remain enough space for gasoline cars.

121711.explanation: The burden on residents must remain limited.

121722.explanation: Scale and village character must be correct.

121673.explanation: A referendum is a very expensive instrument and in light of our municipality's tight finances, D66 prefers other forms of participation.

121674.explanation: We must embrace initiatives from residents who selflessly want to contribute to our municipality, especially regarding green maintenance, which currently generates many complaints.

121677.explanation: We fully support this. The storage of sustainable energy must also be included. However, a financial contribution from the municipality is not always necessary.

121678.explanation: Public space in our municipality is scarce; we do not want a distribution center at the expense of homes and/or the much needed greenery.

121680.explanation: It must be prevented that employees become completely dependent on their employer and can end up on the streets.

121682.explanation: Air quality is a point of attention in our area. We are not in favor of a ban, but support use, provided weather conditions allow it.

121684.explanation: The municipality does not have to organize a fireworks show itself, but must facilitate if associations want to organize a fireworks show or other event. Organizing a fireworks show ourselves puts too much pressure on the budget.

121685.explanation: Not throughout the entire municipality, but for example near the Zonnehofflats in Duivendrecht.

121688.explanation: We do encourage the use of caregiver housing.

121689.explanation: We remain connected to the Youth Fund for Sport and Culture so that children can always participate in sports or cultural activities. We encourage adults to exercise outdoors or through Coherente.

121691.explanation: In Duivendrecht we have two new school buildings, and in Ouderkerk the Hekmanschool has a new building. However, a new school or two new schools must be built for the students of the Kofschip and Amstelschool.

121692.explanation: We arrange this through the Youth Fund for Sport and Culture.

121696.explanation: Additional enforcement officers have already been invested in. We must especially look at where they are deployed. For safety, we prefer preventive, positive interventions such as street coaches and youth workers.

121697.explanation: We want to be very selective here and unobserved through Ouder-Amstel.

121698.explanation: There are sufficient facilities in the area for this.

121701.explanation: Additional housing is desperately needed, but we must also consider the effects on recreational areas and the quality of nature and landscape.

121702.explanation: With exceptions for new urban developments such as the Nieuwe Kern.

121705.explanation: During the last council period we already raised this limit. We must now focus on ensuring that people who need this help actually use it.

121707.explanation: The number of charging points must grow along with the number of electric cars in our municipality.

121709.explanation: The government must ensure good and accessible healthcare. This is a national issue.

121711.explanation: We want to remain financially healthy and live in an attractive municipality. To maintain municipal facilities and a safe and green environment, sufficient budget is needed. Property tax is almost the only way to achieve this.

121712.explanation: This should be a targeted tax, for example for dog waste bags and maintenance of dog parks.

121715.explanation: The current limited availability is not due to refugees, but to insufficient housing construction. And we are currently doing a lot about this.

121719.explanation: Every child must learn to swim, but this is not the responsibility of schools. Through the Youth Fund for Sport and Culture, the municipality makes it possible for every child to learn to swim.

121722.explanation: At specific locations such as Entrada and de Werkstad this is possible. High-rise contributes to affordability and creates more homes and financial possibilities for quality improvements of the environment.

121723.explanation: Some people are specifically looking for individual rooms and they benefit from this option.

121725.explanation: It is expensive enough, but does not need to be cheaper.

121728.explanation: Abolishing it costs more money than keeping it, and the system works well in most places. Around the Zonnehofflats and the Dorpsplein in Duivendrecht, we see problems that must be solved by, for example, periodic bulky waste collection, at least for people who cannot afford this.

121729.explanation: This is a logical route and we want to encourage cycling, but we have doubts about the financial feasibility and desirability.

121673.explanation: We support referenda on important issues. Ouder-Amstel has good participation rules, but on major decisions the opinions of residents must truly count.

121674.explanation: We disagree with management being taken over by residents. Maintenance of facilities and public greenery is a municipal task. We welcome resident initiatives, but always in collaboration with the municipality.

121677.explanation: We are neutral about financial support for residents who want to jointly generate sustainable energy. We prefer to align with national schemes, as this can quickly become costly for a small municipality otherwise.

121678.explanation: This is not an issue in Ouder-Amstel.

121680.explanation: We disagree that employers should arrange the housing of seasonal workers themselves. This is not an issue in our municipality and may lead to undesirable uncontrolled growth of housing, which we want to prevent.

121682.explanation: We believe that open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed. Only if it truly becomes a major problem will we first consult with residents before taking any measures.

121684.explanation: We are neutral towards a municipal fireworks display on New Year's Eve. First, we want to know the costs clearly so we can assess whether it is feasible and responsible for the municipality and its residents.

121685.explanation: We agree that bulky waste should be collected from homes free of charge, but only at fixed times, for example once every two months. This keeps things manageable and the neighborhood tidy.

121688.explanation: We disagree with the statement that farmers may build houses themselves on their land. The rural area should not be overbuilt and the landscape and nature of the Ronde Hoep and the polders must be protected.

121689.explanation: We agree that the municipality can pay for a sports club membership for people receiving social assistance benefits. Sports are very important, and poverty policy must ensure that everyone can participate.

121691.explanation: We are neutral towards extra funding to improve all school buildings. Proper maintenance is important and schools already receive a budget for this. There are no indications that this is not done well in Ouder-Amstel. New constructions of ’t Kofschip and the Amstelschool have higher priority.

121692.explanation: We disagree that every primary school child should automatically receive a subsidy for music lessons. We choose a targeted approach so that children of parents who cannot afford it do get the chance to take music lessons.

121696.explanation: We are neutral towards more municipal enforcement officers (boa’s) for safety. It is important that they can also spend their time on other tasks, such as street litter, parking, and enforcement of general rules.

121697.explanation: We disagree with the statement that there should be more camera surveillance in Ouder-Amstel. There is no major safety problem here that requires it, and residents' privacy must be protected. Enforcement is better done via boa’s and clear regulations.

121698.explanation: We are neutral towards a domestic violence shelter in Ouder-Amstel. What is crucial, however, is attention to domestic violence and femicide. Victims must be safely accommodated and the municipality must actively focus on prevention and support.

121701.explanation: We disagree with the statement that holiday homes should be used as permanent residences. This is not an issue in Ouder-Amstel and is also not desirable for our villages and living environment.

121702.explanation: We agree with the statement that new construction outside the existing village boundaries should be prevented. This preserves the character of the villages, protects the landscape, nature and polders, and prevents unnecessary expansion of infrastructure and facilities.

121705.explanation: We disagree with raising the income threshold for poverty assistance. In Ouder-Amstel, the threshold is already quite high, making support well accessible to those who need it.

121707.explanation: We agree that the municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging spots for electric vehicles. This encourages sustainable transport, makes electric driving practical for residents, and contributes to a cleaner living environment.

121709.explanation: Amstelland Lokaal believes that everyone in Ouder-Amstel has the right to care that aligns with who they are. Care is personal, often vulnerable, and directly relates to someone's identity. Therefore, every resident should be able to choose a care provider who fits their faith, culture, or beliefs. This not only fosters trust and safety but also leads to better care outcomes.

121711.explanation: The property tax (ozb) must be lowered. Local taxes are too high and place an unnecessary burden on residents. Amstelland Lokaal chooses fair, affordable charges so that living in Ouder-Amstel remains accessible for everyone.

121712.explanation: We are in favor of abolishing the dog tax, or transforming it into a purpose tax. The money would then be used to tackle disturbances, clearly indicate dog exercise areas, and make dog waste bags available at supermarkets.

121715.explanation: We are neutral towards priority for refugees with a residence permit in social housing. Housing allocation must be fair, considering residents and national agreements.

121719.explanation: We disagree that the municipality should provide funding for school swimming for all children. We choose a targeted approach so that children of parents who cannot afford it can still participate.

121722.explanation: We disagree with allowing more tall residential buildings in Ouder-Amstel. Our position has always been clear: the village character disappears and the livability of the centers is put under pressure.

121723.explanation: We disagree with a ban on renting out individual rooms. Room rental is important for students, but the focus should be on this, not on uncontrolled growth of housing rentals in the municipality.

121725.explanation: We are neutral towards cheaper paid parking in the centers. Currently, a permit for the blue zone costs about 48 euros. We are definitely not in favor of increasing this.

121728.explanation: We agree that the waste pass should be abolished. The current system does not work well and is expensive to maintain. It is better to invest in easy waste separation so residents can separate waste easily.

121729.explanation: We agree that a new bicycle tunnel should be built between Duivendrecht and De Nieuwe Kern. This improves accessibility and safety for cyclists, especially children and students, with good lighting so the tunnel is always safe.

121673.explanation: For important topics, the residents of Ouder-Amstel are invited during the information gathering phase to express their views through a participation process. Introducing a referendum does not lead to better opinion formation.

121674.explanation: To prevent untidiness, the management of public space should remain under the responsibility of the municipality.

121677.explanation: The CDA Ouder-Amstel wants to promote livability and greening within the municipality. Sustainable energy is part of this. We therefore support initiatives by residents to contribute to sustainability.

121678.explanation: There are sufficient distribution centers within the municipality of Ouder-Amstel. CDA Ouder-Amstel prefers to use the available space for housing construction.

121680.explanation: Agriculture and horticulture contribute to the local economy. It is a shared responsibility of the employer and the municipality to provide suitable housing.

121682.explanation: In recent years, there has been increasing awareness about the generation of fine particulate matter due to the use of fireplaces and wood stoves. Fine particles negatively affect the health of nearby residents. This is undesirable.

121684.explanation: Due to stricter laws and regulations, it is becoming increasingly difficult for private individuals to set off fireworks. The desire to celebrate New Year's Eve remains. A municipality-organized fireworks display meets this need and promotes community spirit.

121685.explanation: Bulky waste and household waste can be directly linked to residents of the municipality. The municipality facilitates, but the polluter must bear the processing costs.

121688.explanation: Provided that predefined conditions regarding size, number, and use are met, farmers should be allowed to build houses themselves on their land.

121689.explanation: Clubs are important for development, relaxation, and socializing. However, CDA Ouder-Amstel does not see it as a primary task of the municipality to pay sports club memberships for people receiving social assistance. Other means exist to support these people.

121691.explanation: Maintenance of school buildings is a shared responsibility between the municipality and the school board. The school board presents a plan. The municipality assesses financial feasibility and can make funds available.

121692.explanation: A primary task of parents is to give their children the opportunity to fully develop. The choice to provide music lessons lies with the parents. In case of insufficient financial means, other provisions are available to support parents.

121696.explanation: Within Ouder-Amstel, the safety of residents is critically reviewed. Agreements have been made to enhance safety. It has been shown that enforcement is necessary. Sufficient boa's must be available for this.

121697.explanation: In places perceived as unsafe, more camera surveillance should be possible. This should be balanced with considerations of safety and privacy protection.

121698.explanation: For residents' safety, it is important that there is a considerable distance between the domestic violence shelter and the original living environment of the resident. Therefore, a domestic violence shelter in Ouder-Amstel will not be inhabited by residents of this municipality.

121701.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for occasional use during part of the year. Permanent residence can lead to undesirable situations.

121702.explanation: New construction outside the existing village boundaries is necessary to meet the enormous demand for new housing. We support initiatives to promote housing development. Office transformation is part of this.

121705.explanation: The municipality applies the national poverty line established by CBS, SCP, and Nibud.

121707.explanation: The increase in the number of electric cars leads to a greater demand for charging points. The number of charging points must keep pace with this increase.

121709.explanation: For people who need care, the primary focus is on recovery. Distinguishing between faith, culture, and beliefs leads to undesirable segregation.

121711.explanation: The property tax (ozb) is currently linked to the market value of the home, using sold homes in the vicinity as a reference. The value of the home and thus the property tax should be linked to the annual inflation.

121712.explanation: The dog tax is not used for cleaning up dog waste. Waste from cats and horses is not taxed.

121715.explanation: The shortage of suitable housing is a major societal problem. Priority for refugees should be assessed on a case-by-case basis based on established criteria.

121719.explanation: The Netherlands is rich in water. From a safety perspective, it is important that people have basic swimming skills. Funding should depend on income.

121722.explanation: When building tall residential flats, the surroundings must be taken into account. Because of environmental impact, not every location is suitable for high-rise flats.

121723.explanation: Room rentals to individuals, such as students, should be possible. Any nuisance caused can be enforced.

121725.explanation: Paid parking (blue zones) is often introduced in town centers to prevent long-term parking by non-residents. Lowering the threshold would encourage undesirable parking.

121728.explanation: Waste collection costs the municipality money. Currently, the municipality has a system in place for waste collection; the waste pass is part of this. As long as there is no better system to make polluters pay, the waste pass remains in use.

121729.explanation: Safety and accessibility are important municipal themes. The necessity of constructing a new bicycle tunnel should be investigated further.

statements.title: The municipality should abolish the waste card.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: Ouder-Amstel should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Residents of Ouder-Amstel should be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Ouder-Amstel moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Ouder-Amstel.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed in Ouder-Amstel.

titleParts.text: In Ouder-Amstel moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: Ouder-Amstel should get a women's shelter (a refuge for victims of domestic violence).

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
Paid parking in the town centers should become cheaper.

titleParts.glossary: Villages and small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
The municipality should allocate money for school swimming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: More high-rise residential towers should be allowed in Ouder-Amstel.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: A new bicycle tunnel should be built between Duivendrecht and De Nieuwe Kern.
